prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Effect of global damp heat ageing on debonding of crosslinke | Statement: 10,000h POE damp heat exposure caused pronounced glass ageing near the interface with partly cohesive failure within glass, attributed to HALS-stabili
2. [SILVER] Source: The Impact of the Lamination Process on the Adhesion Propert | Statement: Relative maintained peel force (RMPF) after 3000h damp heat varies from 15.7% to 73.0% depending on encapsulant and lamination process
3. [SILVER] Source: The Impact of the Lamination Process on the Adhesion Propert | Statement: EVA laminates showed less degradation in peel force after damp heat than POE samples across tested lamination temperatures
4. [SILVER] Source: The Impact of the Lamination Process on the Adhesion Propert | Statement: Higher lamination temperature was beneficial for peel force and gel content before and after aging for cross-linking encapsulants like EVA and POE
5. [SILVER] Source: The Impact of the Lamination Process on the Adhesion Propert | Statement: TPO encapsulant had consistent peel force of about 160 N/cm independent of peak lamination temperature
